titleStmt.title: Letter from Raymond Smyth to Under Secretary's Office, 9 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Raymond Smyth

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

correspAction.placeName: Land Registry of Ireland, Central Office, Four Courts, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
In Reply, please quote
Folio Number or other refe-
ference as given below. CHIEF SECRETARY'S
11416- - 4 JUL 1916
LAND REGISTRY OF IRELAND,
CENTRAL OFFICE,
FOUR COURTS,
DUBLIN. 9th May, 1916. Sir, Acting on behalf of the Registrar,
I transmit herewith, for the information of the
Lord Lieutenant, the accompanying 78 Statements
by the members of the staff of the Land Registry
at present employed in Dublin (totalling 82), as
requested in the circular dated 1st May. Statements have not been obtained from
the four following, who, for the sufficient reasons
shown below have been as yet unable to resume duty
at the office:—
Names. Reasons.
H. Mooney Ill.
J.A.J. Palmer Ill.
W. Webster. In Cork.
Pensioner- Messenger O'Toole On Military Service I am, Sir,
Your obedient Servant
Raymond Smyth
Chief Clerk. The Under Secretary for Ireland,
Dublin Castle
